ddls:
CREATE TABLE "materials" (
  "material_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each material */,
  "material_name" TEXT /* Name of the material */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the material */,
  "base_unit" TEXT /* Base unit of measure for the material */,
  "total_stock" INTEGER /* Total stock available for the material */,
  "special_stock" TEXT /* Type of special stock, if any */,
  "available_stock" INTEGER /* Available stock for use in production */,
  "storage_bin" TEXT /* Storage bin where the material is kept */,
  "storage_type" TEXT /* Type of storage for the material (e.g., regular, consignment) */,
  "plant" TEXT /* Plant location where the material is stored */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the material (e.g., raw material, component) */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supplier providing the material */,
  "min_stock_level" INTEGER /* Minimum stock level to trigger reordering */,
  "max_stock_level" INTEGER /* Maximum stock level to avoid overstocking */,
  "lead_time" INTEGER /* Lead time for material replenishment */,
  "cost_per_unit" TEXT /* Cost per unit for financial tracking */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Expiration date for perishable materials */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("material_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_materials_supplier_id FOREIGN KEY ("supplier_id") REFERENCES suppliers ("supplier_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_materials_storage_bin FOREIGN KEY ("storage_bin") REFERENCES storage_locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_materials_plant FOREIGN KEY ("plant") REFERENCES plants ("plant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "batches" (
  "batch_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each batch */,
  "material_id" INTEGER /* ID of the material associated with the batch */,
  "batch_number" TEXT /* Batch number for tracking purposes */,
  "gr_date" TEXT /* Date when the goods were received */,
  "transfer_order_number" INTEGER /* Transfer order number associated with the goods receipt */,
  "gr_number" INTEGER /* Goods receipt number for the batch */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Expiration date for the batch */,
  "manufacturing_date" TEXT /* Date when the batch was manufactured */,
  "quality_status" TEXT /* Quality status of the batch (e.g., approved, rejected) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("batch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_batches_material_id FOREIGN KEY ("material_id") REFERENCES materials ("material_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "storage_locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each storage location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the storage location */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the storage location */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum storage capacity of the location */,
  "current_occupancy" INTEGER /* Current occupancy of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "plants" (
  "plant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each plant */,
  "plant_name" TEXT /* Name of the plant */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the plant */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the plant manager */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number for the plant */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("plant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stock_movements" (
  "movement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each stock movement */,
  "material_id" INTEGER /* ID of the material being moved */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of material moved */,
  "movement_type" TEXT /* Type of movement (e.g., receipt, consumption) */,
  "movement_date" TEXT /* Date of the movement */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who initiated the movement */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the stock movement (e.g., production, repair) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_stock_movements_material_id FOREIGN KEY ("material_id") REFERENCES materials ("material_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_stock_movements_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "suppliers" (
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each supplier */,
  "supplier_name" TEXT /* Name of the supplier */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Contact person at the supplier */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number for the supplier */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address for the supplier */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the supplier */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("supplier_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for login */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, manager, operator) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
